title: Câteva reproșuri și unele speranțe în tabăra votanților lui N. Dan
summary: Political analyst Cristian Preda examines the growing discontent among President Nicușor Dan's voter base, identifying eight distinct groups ranging from loyal supporters to radical critics. The article catalogs grievances including Dan's perceived closeness to sovereignists and PSD, his failure to appoint intelligence service chiefs, lack of vocal support for PM Bolojan, and general passivity, while noting that some voters remain patient and supportive.
talking_points: A Facebook post about losing confidence in President Dan garnered 80,000 views and 600+ comments in 24 hours, revealing widespread voter frustration
Eight distinct voter groups are identified, from loyal supporters urging patience to radical critics questioning whether Dan even wants to be president
Key grievances include Dan's rapprochement with sovereignists/PSD, failure to hold a justice referendum, not appointing intelligence service chiefs, and insufficient support for PM Bolojan
Some voters fear Dan will repeat Iohannis's pattern of disappointing supporters or becoming captive to the SRI intelligence service
Despite growing difficulty convincing those around him, the author maintains support for the president's actions
author: Cristian Preda
